summary: Correct README to reflect two-ROM structure and CFW lineage found in reference dumps
description: Extracting reference/ revealed: (1) the M1000 has two ROMs — a 32 KB OS ROM (27256) and a separate 64 KB patch ROM (27512), not a 32 KB patch region; (2) the CFW images present are Nordcore (v1.13/1.14) and GliGli (v1.16), a different lineage than the Tauntek v1.20/1.21 the README credits. Update README §4 and §5, the §1 patch-storage row, and reference the organized reference/extracted/ tree and the v1.14 byte-patch map.

summary: Initialize git repository and make initial commit
description: Make the project a git repo: git init on main, add .gitignore (.DS_Store etc.), and create the initial commit containing README.md, issues.jsonl, and the reference/ material (original zips + organized reference/extracted/ tree).

summary: Stand up Ghidra-based 6809 RE pipeline + asm6809 round-trip validation
description: Foundation for all firmware docs. Toolchain (user-chosen): Ghidra + a community 6809 SLEIGH processor module as the primary analysis brain (decompiler -> readable pseudocode for the prose docs); asm6809 2.17 (installed) as the byte-identical round-trip correctness gate; emulator/MAME trace reserved for dynamic analysis when control flow is murky. Import the v1.11 OS ROM (reference/extracted/matrix-1000/os-rom/v1.11/M1000-CAT27256P-17_v111.BIN) as raw 6809, base $8000. Seed entry points RESET=$8003, IRQ=$85E3, FIRQ=$84B4 + string-referencing routines; iteratively separate code from data/tables. HARD GATE (already prototyped): an asm6809 source of the image re-assembles byte-identical (md5 3e0280ce) to the ROM; keep this true as data runs become instructions. Document the reproducible pipeline in docs/disassembly/workflow.md.

summary: Hardware: memory map + I/O map, verified against code
description: Document the 6809 64K memory map (RAM, OS ROM $8000-$FFFF, patch ROM, I/O decode region, vector table) and the I/O map (68B50 ACIA, AM6012 DAC, mux latches, 8254-class timer, panel ports) with addresses and per-register bit meanings. Derive from Bob Grieb's redrawn schematics + bit-hack/OberheimMatrix1000 memory map + datasheets, then CONFIRM every I/O address against actual load/store operands in the disassembly. Discrepancies noted, not hidden. Every firmware doc depends on this.

summary: Firmware macro-architecture overview (flagship doc)
description: The centerpiece deliverable. Prose explanation of how the firmware operates at a macro level: boot/init -> foreground main loop -> the IRQ-driven ~50 Hz scheduler that runs the voice engine, mod-matrix evaluation, and DAC/mux scan-out; plus where MIDI, patch load/save, autotune and self-test sit. High-level map that the subsystem docs (#6-#12) drill into. Prose + small diagrams/pseudocode, citing addresses.

summary: Firmware: boot/init path + RAM (zero-page/work) map
description: Trace the RESET=$8003 path: RAM clear/init, zero-page variable layout, hardware init (ACIA, timer, DAC/mux, panel), jump into the main loop. Produce docs/firmware/boot-init.md and docs/firmware/ram-map.md (the firmware's variable map), which later subsystem docs reference by name.

summary: Firmware: main loop & IRQ/FIRQ scheduler (the 50 Hz tick)
description: Document the split between the foreground loop and the interrupt handlers (IRQ=$85E3, FIRQ=$84B4): what the 8254-class timer tick drives, the ~50 Hz/20 ms update cadence, and how work is dispatched each tick. This is the heartbeat the voice engine and mod-matrix hang off of.

summary: Firmware: voice engine (per-tick CV computation + DAC/mux scan-out)
description: How the firmware computes per-voice control values each tick and scans them out to the six CEM3396 voices through the single AM6012 DAC + 4051/4053 multiplexers + sample-and-hold. The core real-time CV delivery routine.

summary: Firmware: modulation matrix storage + evaluation
description: How patch modulation routings are stored, when/how the matrix is rebuilt (the GliGli speed-up touched this), and how sources->destinations are evaluated each tick to feed the voice engine. 20 user routings, ~20 sources, ~32 destinations.

summary: Firmware: MIDI handling (68B50 ACIA RX/TX, SysEx, NRPN/CC edit)
description: Document the ACIA-driven MIDI path: receive/transmit, running status, note/voice assignment, SysEx (patch dump/request, the 80<->134-byte transfer), and real-time NRPN/CC parameter edit. The MIDI-under-load bottleneck and what the buffering looks like.

summary: Firmware: patch storage + the 80<->134-byte pack/unpack routine
description: RAM patch buffer, patch load/save (user RAM and from patch ROM), and crucially the pack/unpack routine that maps the packed ~80-byte stored record to the 134-byte internal patch format. This routine is the authoritative key for the patch-ROM decoding track (#15-#18).

summary: Firmware: autotune / calibrate / power-on self-test
description: Document CALIBRATE and FINE TUNE routines (oscillator/filter CV calibration against the pitch reference) and the power-on self-test behind the BAD OSC/BAD VCF/BAD RES/BAD WAVE strings (~$B1C4): what it measures and how it flags bad voices.

summary: Hardware deep-dive: DAC/CV-mux + voice architecture
description: The CV delivery engine in hardware detail: AM6012 DAC, 4051/4053 analog mux tree, sample-and-hold per parameter/voice, and the CEM3396 voice (2 DCOs + waveshaper + multimode VCF + 2 VCAs) and how CV/gate reach each of the 6 voices. Pairs with firmware #8.

summary: Hardware deep-dive: MIDI/ACIA + clocks/timing + panel/display
description: Supporting hardware docs: 68B50 ACIA wiring/baud/IRQ; crystal, 6809 E-clock, DCO clock dividers and the 50 Hz tick source; front-panel switches, LEDs and the display. Confirm part numbers and mapped addresses from schematics + code.

summary: Patch ROM: packed record format (the ~80-byte ROM layout)
description: Side track. Determine the exact packed record size and byte layout of the factory patch ROM (reference/extracted/matrix-1000/patch-rom/M1000-PatchROM_27512_C2DB.BIN, 64 KB, ~800 patches). Derive authoritatively from the firmware unpack routine (#11) and cross-check empirically (name strides etc.).

summary: Patch ROM: 134-byte internal parameter map
description: Document the full ordered 134-byte internal patch parameter map (DCO1/2, VCF, VCA, 3 DADSR envelopes, 2 LFOs, 2 ramps, tracking generator, 10 matrix-mod buses) with value ranges. Cross-reference CMU m6-patch table and the youngmonkey Matrix-1000 SysEx doc; ground-truth against firmware usage.

summary: Patch ROM: reproducible decoder + decode all 800 factory patches
description: A reproducible recipe/script (byte offsets -> human-readable patch) that decodes every factory patch from the patch ROM. Validate by confirming patch names and a few parameter values against an existing editor / known patch list; the pack/unpack round-trips.

summary: Patch ROM: sound-design notes/statistics across the 800 factory patches
description: The side-goal payoff: mine the decoded factory set for sound-design insight (common envelope/LFO settings, filter usage, matrix-routing idioms, oscillator setups) and write it up so others can learn from the factory programmers.

summary: Add a Roadmap section to the README
description: Add a Roadmap to README.md summarizing the documentation goal (human-readable technical docs derived from disassembly + patch-ROM mining), the planned docs/ tree, and the prioritized issue tracks (foundation -> hardware -> firmware subsystems; parallel patch-rom track). Point readers at issues.jsonl as the live backlog.

summary: Docs index, sources/licensing, and open license for the docs
description: docs/README.md (index + reading order), docs/sources.md (references + per-source license terms; cite prior art, keep our prose original), and choose/apply an open license for the documentation (proposing CC BY 4.0) so anyone can reuse. Link docs/ from the top-level README.

summary: Holistic architecture: SVG diagrams (system + memory map) and a tying-it-together doc
description: Add a single holistic view of the whole machine: an SVG system/data-flow diagram (CPU, memory, I/O, the two interrupt paths, the DAC/mux/S&H -> CEM3396 voice chain, DCO clocks, MIDI, panel) and a visual memory-map SVG, plus docs/architecture.md narrative tying the subsystems together and a firmware-rewrite/Forth-feasibility note. Link from docs/README.md.

summary: Wire patchbook names into the patch decoder and analysis
description: Parse the Don Solaris patchbook into names-by-number (tools/patch_names.py), join into decode_patches.py (record r = patch r+200) so the bulk CSV is named+labelled, and surface named exemplars in analyze_patches.py / sound-design-notes.md.

summary: Force-directed factory patch map SVG (brightness x register, by genre)
description: Visualize all factory patches in a brightness x register space from decoded parameters, coloured by heuristic genre from names. tools/patch_genre.py (classifier), tools/patch_map_svg.py (rank-normalize + force relaxation + SVG), docs/diagrams/patch-map.svg. Report dominant genres.

summary: Weave Owner's Manual context into the reverse-engineered docs
description: Add short 'what the user sees <-> what the firmware does' bridges citing manual pages: Ext. Funct (functions 0-7) into boot-init/main-loop; CALIBRATE/Fine Tune into autotune-selftest (#12); Data Dump into midi-handling/patch-storage; Group Mode (Units); MIDI implementation chart cross-check. Manual is reference/OberheimMatrix1000.pdf.

summary: Add CEM3396 datasheet to references and correct analog docs against it
description: Download the CEM3396 datasheet into reference/, cite in sources.md, and improve accuracy of voice-architecture.md and dac-cv-mux.md (VCA inventory, VCF modes/poles, and crucially the chip's microprocessor CV control interface - internal S&H/addressing vs external 4051/4053, which our DAC/mux doc may have wrong).
